seoTitle: How to Add Page Numbers in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Add and customize page numbers in Google Docs, skip numbering on your cover page, and set custom start numbers for polished multi-page documents.
intro: Manually tracking page order in a long document is a waste of time when Google Docs can handle it automatically. This guide covers inserting page numbers through the Insert menu, choosing between header and footer placement, and skipping numbering on your title page. You'll also see how to set custom starting numbers for more advanced document structures.

steps.heading: Open the Page numbers panel
steps.body: Go to Insert, then Page numbers, to open the configuration panel where you'll choose your placement and style.

steps.heading: Choose header or footer placement
steps.body: Pick a standard preset, such as top-right header or bottom-right footer, depending on how you want your numbering to appear.

steps.heading: Skip numbering on the first page
steps.body: Turn on Different first page so your title page or cover sheet doesn't display a page number while the rest of the document continues sequentially.

steps.heading: Set a custom start number
steps.body: Open More options in the Page numbers dialog to define a custom starting number or restrict numbering to a specific section.

steps.heading: Style your page numbers
steps.body: Adjust the font size, color, and alignment of the page number text so it complements the rest of your document's typography.

faq.question: How do I hide the page number on my title page only?
faq.answer: Enable the Different first page setting in your header or footer options, which removes numbering from just the first page while keeping it on the rest.

faq.question: Can I start page numbering from a number other than 1?
faq.answer: Yes, open More options under Page numbers and set your desired starting number for the section.

faq.question: Do page numbers update automatically if I add or remove content?
faq.answer: Yes, page numbers recalculate automatically as your document's length changes, so you don't need to update them manually.

faq.question: Can I place page numbers in the top left instead of the top right?
faq.answer: The built-in presets focus on standard header and footer corners, but you can manually reposition the number text box if you need a different placement.
